turns.user_message: My friend kept telling me how amazing this weekend was supposed to be, but I just sat there nodding along while feeling completely empty inside. It has been like this for a few weeks now, and I really do not know what changed. I tried to force a smile when the music started, but my face just would not cooperate. Everything feels so flat and distant lately, like I am watching a movie through thick glass. I keep waiting for that familiar spark to return, but it just stays buried under a heavy blanket of nothing.

They keep asking why I am so quiet lately, and honestly I just do not have the energy to explain that the usual things stopped working. I go through the exact same routines every single morning, but the colors seem completely washed out now. I used to get genuinely excited about simple plans, but now they just feel like heavy chores I have to drag myself through. The silence in my head is so loud that I barely notice what is happening around me anymore. It is exhausting pretending to care when everything just feels completely hollow.

I still hear their voice echoing in my head whenever they tell me to just snap out of it, but I genuinely cannot find the switch anymore. Every single day blends into a long stretch of gray where nothing registers as important or interesting. I try to grab onto little moments, but they slip right through my fingers before I can even process them. The emptiness has grown so heavy that I barely recognize the person looking back at me in the mirror. I am just floating through time while everything else moves forward without me.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something someone else said or did. This has been going on for a while.
